content: Six ways to divide England linguistically — fascinating visualization by @starkeycomics showing regional language patterns across the country.
The beauty of the fediverse is that no single company owns your social graph. Platforms like Mastodon prove social media can be decentralized and community-driven.
Spent the weekend rewriting a React component and realized the simplest solution was just using derived state instead of useRef. Sometimes the boring solution is the best solution.
Satellite data from the past decade shows accelerating glacier retreat in multiple mountain regions. The long-term impacts on freshwater availability could be severe.
Interesting new paper exploring how smaller language models can outperform large ones in domain-specific tasks when properly fine-tuned.
Good UI design is mostly about consistency. If users can predict what will happen before they click, you've already won half the battle.
Whenever you hear "ban for kids" online, read it as "ID collection by a third-party company that will probably misuse or leak the data." Policies often sound good but hide surveillance risks.
